contents:
# Russian superyacht Nord breaches Hormuz maritime blockade from Dubai to Oman

## Overview
The 142-meter Russian superyacht Nord, linked to sanctioned billionaire Alexey Mordashov, crossed the Strait of Hormuz amid an ongoing maritime blockade. It departed Dubai on Friday and anchored in Muscat by Sunday morning without incident, while commercial traffic nearly halted and oil prices hit $120 per barrel.[1]

## Details
The vessel sailed from Dubai on April 24, 2026, transiting the strait overnight. U.S. Marines remain actively boarding vessels in the zone, raising questions on how the $500M yacht passed unchallenged. The event occurred as Iran added new measures to the Hormuz blockade from the Arabian Sea direction. Iranian Navy Commander Shahram Irani announced the additional restrictions. No casualties or disruptions reported on Nord itself. Commercial shipping has trickled to near zero.[1]

**Location**: Strait of Hormuz, Dubai to Muscat, Oman

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @gCaptain, which covers maritime news, vessel casualties, port disruptions, shipping incidents. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following maritime news will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
